content.text:
Now I have a clear view of the molecule. Looking at atom S:9 (the yellow sulfur atom), I can see it's connected to:

1. C:10 (Carbon, index 10) - directly bonded
2. C:3 (Carbon, index 3) - directly bonded

From the 3D visualization, I can confirm that sulfur atom 9 has exactly two covalent bonds - one to carbon atom 10 and one to carbon atom 3. There are no other atoms directly bonded to the sulfur atom.

The geomconf_index for this molecule is 5871, as indicated by the filename "geomconf_5871.xyz".

Therefore, the neighbor atoms of atom 9 (S) are:
- Atom index 10, element C
- Atom index 3, element C

Geomconf_index: 5871

question: Please find the neighbour atoms of the atom with atomic index 9, whose element should be S. Here, neighbour atoms are defined as atoms that are covalently connected to this atom. Report all neighbour atoms by listing their atomic index (as shown in the image) and their element. Also return the geomconf_index for this molecule.
